title: tragopogon elatior
intro: Tragopogon elatior, also known as the horse thistle or common knapweed, is a biennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its tall stem, purple flowers, and prickly leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Tragopogon elatior include its ability to lower fever, reduce swelling, and aid in the treatment of wounds and skin irritations. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
